content:
We spend our careers learning how to calculate ROI.

Maybe it's time we calculated another one.

Return on Integrity.

How much trust did you earn this year?

How many people would recommend working with you when you're not in the room?

How many opportunities came because of your reputation instead of your résumé?

Money compounds.

So does credibility.

The difference is...

One can be lost overnight.

The other keeps paying dividends for decades.
comment: Return on integrity is real, but in large orgs it’s often aggregated at brand level until a failure surfaces individual gaps, which can distort day-to-day incentives.
